question.text: In India, dividend income is taxed:

answer.text: In the hands of the company paying it (DDT)
In the hands of the shareholders receiving it, with TDS of 10% on dividend above Rs.5,000
It is completely tax-free for all shareholders
Only NRI shareholders pay tax on dividends

explanation.text: **The Logic:** From AY 2021-22, dividend is taxable in hands of shareholders. Company deducts 10% TDS on dividend exceeding Rs.5,000 while crediting to shareholder accounts. **The Trap:** DDT (paid by company) was abolished. Now shareholders pay tax at their applicable slab rates. **Cross-Exam Context:** Dividend taxation tested in NISM XV Ch 9. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** For high-income shareholders (30% tax bracket), dividend income is less tax-efficient than capital gains.
